questions.q: What is the primary function of a URL?
opts: (A) To store files on the web
(B) To locate web pages on the internet
questions.explanation: A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is a web address used to locate a specific resource on the internet.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of HTTP request?
questions.explanation: Both POST and GET are types of HTTP requests. POST is used for creating new resources, while GET is used for retrieving existing resources.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a web browser?
opts: (A) To store files on the web
(B) To locate web pages on the internet
questions.explanation: A web browser's primary function is to display HTML content, allowing users to view and interact with web pages.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a secure HTTP connection?
questions.explanation: A secure HTTP connection, also known as HTTPS, uses the 'https://' protocol to encrypt data and ensure secure communication between the client and server.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a web server?
opts: (A) To store files on the web
(B) To locate web pages on the internet
(C) To serve web pages to clients
questions.explanation: A web server's primary function is to serve web pages to clients, responding to HTTP requests and providing access to hosted resources.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of web framework?
questions.explanation: Both Django and Flask are popular web frameworks used for building web applications. Django is a high-level framework, while Flask is a microframework.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a client-side script?
opts: (C) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: Client-side scripts, typically written in JavaScript, are used to enhance user experience by adding interactive elements and dynamic effects to web pages.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of HTTP header?
questions.explanation: Both Authorization and Accept-Language are types of HTTP headers. Authorization headers are used for authentication, while Accept-Language headers specify the user's preferred language.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a web cache?
opts: (D) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: A web cache stores frequently accessed resources, reducing the need for repeated requests to the server and improving page load times.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of web security vulnerability?
questions.explanation: Both SQL Injection and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) are types of web security vulnerabilities. SQL Injection involves injecting malicious SQL code, while XSS involves injecting malicious JavaScript code.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a web proxy?
opts: (C) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: A web proxy filters network traffic, allowing administrators to control access to specific resources and improve security.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a RESTful API?
opts: (A) It uses a fixed endpoint for all requests
(B) It uses a resource-based endpoint structure
(C) It does not support stateless communication
questions.explanation: A RESTful API uses a resource-based endpoint structure, making it easier to identify and access specific resources.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a web service?
opts: (A) To provide a user interface for a web application
(B) To expose a web application's functionality as a service
(C) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: A web service exposes a web application's functionality as a service, allowing other applications to access and use its resources.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of web architecture pattern?
questions.explanation: Both Model-View-Controller (MVC) and Model-View-ViewModel (MVVM) are types of web architecture patterns. MVC separates concerns into three interconnected components, while MVVM separates concerns into four interconnected components.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a microservices architecture?
opts: (D) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: A microservices architecture improves scalability and flexibility by breaking down a monolithic application into smaller, independent services.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of web performance optimization technique?
questions.explanation: Both code splitting and image compression are types of web performance optimization techniques. Code splitting involves breaking down large codebases into smaller chunks, while image compression reduces the file size of images.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a service-oriented architecture (SOA)?
opts: (D) To expose a web application's functionality as a service
questions.explanation: A service-oriented architecture (SOA) exposes a web application's functionality as a service, allowing other applications to access and use its resources.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of web security threat?
questions.explanation: Both SQL Injection and Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) are types of web security threats. SQL Injection involves injecting malicious SQL code, while CSRF involves tricking users into performing unintended actions.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a load balancer?
opts: (A) To distribute incoming traffic across multiple servers
(D) To store data on the server
questions.explanation: A load balancer distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers, improving scalability and reliability by ensuring that no single server becomes overwhelmed.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a cloud-based web application?
opts: (B) It uses a scalable and flexible infrastructure
(C) It does not support cloud computing
(D) It uses a traditional on-premises infrastructure
questions.explanation: A cloud-based web application uses a scalable and flexible infrastructure, allowing it to easily adapt to changing demands and scale up or down as needed.
